title: Emoji Type
description: Type Minecraft emojis and symbols with simple shortcodes.
body:
#
🙂 Emoji Type
###
**Type Minecraft emojis and symbols with simple shortcodes.**
**Emoji Type** allows you to instantly insert 200+ emojis into your game using intuitive keywords. Type `:skull:` and get ☠️ instantly. Works in chat, signs, books, anvils - everywhere text appears. No resource packs or texture changes needed. --- ## 🚀 Key Features * **Seamless Integration:** Works in chat, signs, books, and even anvils. * **Smart Autocomplete:** Type a colon (`:`) in chat to see a real-time list of suggestions. * **Vanilla Compatible:** Emojis are standard Unicode. Other players can see your emojis even if they *don't* have the mod installed! * **Emoji Pack:** Create, edit your own emoji packs via a built-in visual editor. ### Bundled Emoji Packs | Pack | Description | Examples | | --- | --- | --- | | **Minecraft Essentials** | Tools, weapons, and status icons. | ⚔️ ⛏️ 🏹 ☀️ ❄️ | | **Text Art (Kaomoji)** | Classic ASCII and Japanese emoticons. | `¯\_(ツ)_/¯` `(╯°□°)╯` | | **Letter Emojis** | Stylized circled alphabets. | ⓐ Ⓑ ⓒ Ⓓ | --- ## 🛠️ How to Use 1. **Install:** Drop the mod into your `mods` folder (Client-side only!). 2. **Trigger:** Press `:` in any text field. 3. **Search:** Start typing (e.g., `:sw...`) and use your arrow keys or mouse to select. 4. **Anywhere Else:** On signs or anvils, simply type the full code (e.g., `:heart:`) and watch it transform. > **Customizing:** Access the config via **Mod Menu (Fabric)** or the **Mods setting (NeoForge)** to enable/disable emoji packs or build your own from scratch! --- ## ❓ Frequently Asked Questions **Do I need this on my server?**
Nope! It’s **100% client-side**. It won’t do anything if installed on a server. **Can my friends see the emojis?**
Yes! Since these are native Unicode characters, anyone on a modern Minecraft client can see them perfectly. **How do I share my custom packs?**
Just send the `.yaml` file found in `config/emojitype/emoji_packs/`. Your friends can just drop it in their folder and they're good to go. **Why no autocomplete on signs?**
Minecraft’s sign and anvil interfaces are a bit more restrictive than chat. For now, just type the full keyword (like `:check:`) and it will convert automatically. ## 🖼️ Gallery | Chat Suggestions | Config Screen | Emoji Packs | | :---: | :---: | :---: | | | | | **View more images in the Modrinth's gallery...**
